title: Mumbai Sev Puri

ingredients.name: mini crispy puri shells (store-bought, or homemade from rava)

subs.name: store-bought rice-flour puri shells make the dish gluten-free; they hold up slightly longer before softening
2 tablespoons of ready tamarind concentrate thinned with 1 tablespoon water and sweetened with a pinch of sugar
thick coconut yogurt for a dairy-free version; the tangy note carries well

steps.text: Boil the potatoes in a saucepan of salted water over medium-high heat until completely tender, about 15 minutes. Drain, peel while still warm, and mash coarsely with a fork. Season with a pinch of salt, half the chaat masala, and the red chilli powder. Set aside.

Make the tamarind chutney: strain the tamarind pulp through a sieve into a small saucepan, pressing firmly to extract all the liquid. Add jaggery and a pinch of salt. Cook over low heat, stirring, for 4–5 minutes until the chutney thickens and coats the back of a spoon. Cool completely.

Make the green chutney by blending mint leaves, coriander leaves, green chilli, lemon juice, and a pinch of salt with just enough water (about 3 tablespoons) to get a smooth, pourable consistency. Taste and adjust salt.

Arrange the puri shells on a large flat plate or tray. Place a small teaspoon of spiced mashed potato into each puri. Top with a pinch of chopped onion and chopped tomato, pressing lightly so everything stays in place.

Drizzle tamarind chutney and green chutney generously over all the topped puris. Add a small spoonful of whisked curd if using. Finish with a heavy shower of fine sev so each puri is well covered. Sprinkle the remaining chaat masala on top and serve immediately — sev puri must be eaten within two minutes of assembly or the puris go soft.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: rava → store-bought rice-flour puri shells make the dish gluten-free; they hold up slightly longer before softening; tamarind → 2 tablespoons of ready tamarind concentrate thinned with 1 tablespoon water and sweetened with a pinch of sugar; curd → thick coconut yogurt for a dairy-free version; the tangy note carries well
